Clone the TensorFlow models repository from https://github.com/tensorflow/models.git
Navigate to the 'docs/vision' directory containing the 'object_detection.ipynb' notebook
Set up a Python environment with TensorFlow 2.12 installed (e.g., via pip install tensorflow==2.12)
Open the 'object_detection.ipynb' notebook in Google Colab or a Jupyter environment with the configured TensorFlow 2.12 environment
Run all notebook cells sequentially until reaching the cell labeled 'Import required libraries from tensorflow models as image below'
In that cell, execute the import commands exactly as given in the notebook which typically include imports from 'object_detection' module under 'models/research'
Specifically observe the import statements that import from 'object_detection.utils' and 'object_detection.models' (e.g., from object_detection.utils import visualization_utils as vis_utils)
Note that the import step fails with an error (e.g., ModuleNotFoundError or ImportError) indicating that the 'object_detection' module cannot be found or has missing dependencies
Capture the exact error message and traceback produced during the import failure
Validate that no further cells can be executed due to missing dependencies or failed import
Optionally, verify that the error persists with the default TensorFlow 2.12 environment without additional manual installation of 'object_detection' dependencies
Assert that the import failure reproducibly occurs on a clean environment with only TensorFlow 2.12 installed and the notebook code unmodified
